# Seller-side template: Florida vacant-land purchase custom terms

Purpose: attorney/title-company review draft for the seller-side contract package used in buyer-first vacant-land assignment deals.

> **Not legal advice. Do not send to sellers as-is.** This is an operating template that must be reviewed by a Florida real-estate attorney and the Florida closing/title company before use. It is designed to sit beside a Florida vacant-land purchase agreement such as Florida Realtors VAC-14, not to replace state-required forms.

## Source model

Transcript control stack:

- assignable seller purchase contract;
- feasibility / due-diligence period running to closing;
- nominal earnest money, preferably due at closing rather than upfront;
- termination by written notice/email before closing;
- 30–45 day contractual closing window, with operational target of 2–3 weeks;
- title-company closing;
- builder/end-buyer funds earnest money on the assignment side.

## Recommended base form

For Florida / Lee County / Lehigh Acres:

- Use **Florida Realtors VAC-14 Vacant Land Contract** or attorney-approved Florida vacant-land purchase contract.
- Do not reproduce copyrighted state association forms into this repo.
- Use this file as the custom-terms/addendum/checklist for counsel.

## Deal variables

```text
Seller(s): [SELLER LEGAL NAME(S)]
Buyer: [OKEITO ENTITY / BUYER ENTITY]
Property address / parcel ID: [PROPERTY]
County/state: Lee County, Florida
Purchase price: $[SELLER_PRICE]
Initial deposit: $250, or $[AMOUNT]
Closing date: [30–45 DAYS FROM EFFECTIVE DATE]
Closing agent/title company: [TITLE COMPANY]
Buyer intended use: investment / assignment / resale / vacant residential land due diligence
Due diligence period: through and including the Closing Date, unless waived earlier in writing
Assignment: permitted
Notices by email: permitted if attorney approves
```

## VAC-14 fill strategy for attorney review

### 1. Assignability

On VAC-14 Paragraph 7, choose one of the **may assign** options, not “may not assign.”

Preferred operating position:

```text
Buyer may assign this Contract and Buyer’s rights under this Contract.
```

Attorney/title-company decision needed:

- Option A: Buyer may assign and be released from further liability.
- Option B: Buyer may assign but remains liable unless released.

Safer practical fallback is often Option B unless title/counsel approves full release.

### 2. Due Diligence / Feasibility Period

Operating intent:

```text
The Due Diligence Period runs through and including the Closing Date unless Buyer waives it in writing. During the Due Diligence Period, Buyer may determine, in Buyer’s sole and absolute discretion, whether the Property is suitable for Buyer’s intended use, including but not limited to title, access, survey, wetlands, zoning, utilities, buildability, environmental condition, marketability, buyer/investor demand, and assignment/resale feasibility.
```

Termination concept:

```text
Buyer may terminate this Contract during the Due Diligence Period by written notice to Seller. Upon termination, Buyer and Seller will have no further obligations except obligations that expressly survive termination.
```

Counsel must confirm whether “through closing” is enforceable / acceptable in Florida and whether it conflicts with the base form.

### 3. Earnest money / deposit timing

Transcript-style operating intent:

```text
Initial deposit: $250. Deposit shall be delivered to Escrow Agent at closing, unless Buyer elects to deliver earlier or Escrow Agent requires earlier delivery in writing.
```

Fallback ladder if seller/title company refuses:

1. Deposit due at closing.
2. Deposit due only after end-buyer assignment and builder deposit.
3. Small refundable deposit due within 3 business days.
4. Walk away if seller requires meaningful non-refundable earnest money before builder validation.

### 4. Closing date

```text
Closing shall occur on or before [DATE], approximately 30–45 days after Effective Date, unless extended by written agreement of the parties.
```

Operating rule: even though the contract window is 30–45 days, builder go/no-go should happen within 2–24 hours and typical closing target should be 2–3 weeks.

### 5. Buyer-as-principal / no broker disclosure

Addendum concept:

```text
Buyer is entering this Contract as a principal for Buyer’s own investment purposes. Buyer is not acting as Seller’s real estate broker, agent, fiduciary, or advisor. Seller may seek independent legal, tax, financial, or real-estate advice before signing.
```

### 6. Assignment / resale disclosure

Addendum concept:

```text
Seller acknowledges that Buyer may assign this Contract, sell Buyer’s contractual rights, close directly, double close, or otherwise transfer Buyer’s interest in the transaction to another buyer or entity. Seller acknowledges that Buyer may receive an assignment fee, profit, or other compensation from such assignment, resale, or transfer.
```

Florida counsel must confirm whether additional wholesaling disclosures are required or recommended.

### 7. Title and closing-agent instructions

```text
Closing shall be handled by [TITLE COMPANY / CLOSING AGENT], which must be authorized to close Florida vacant-land transactions. Closing Agent is authorized to receive purchase funds, deposits, assignment-related documents, and disburse proceeds and approved assignment fees on the settlement statement as permitted by law and underwriting/title requirements.
```

### 8. Notices / termination email

```text
Notices may be delivered by email to the addresses listed below, in addition to any notice methods required by the base contract. A termination notice delivered by email during the Due Diligence Period shall be effective upon delivery if permitted by the base contract and applicable law.
```

Counsel/title must confirm this against VAC-14 notice requirements.

## Seller-side packet checklist

- [ ] Base Florida vacant-land purchase agreement.
- [ ] Assignability option selected correctly.
- [ ] Due diligence period set through closing or attorney-approved equivalent.
- [ ] Deposit timing drafted clearly.
- [ ] Buyer-as-principal disclosure attached.
- [ ] Assignment/resale disclosure attached.
- [ ] Title company named or TBD language approved.
- [ ] Email notice/termination mechanics approved.
- [ ] Seller contact details and email captured.
- [ ] Parcel ID, legal description, and owner names verified against county/property appraiser records.

## Attorney questions

1. Can the Due Diligence Period validly run through the Closing Date in Florida vacant-land deals?
2. Can the deposit be stated but not due until closing?
3. If deposit is not delivered until closing, is there consideration/enforceability risk under Florida law?
4. Which VAC-14 assignment option should be selected for wholesale assignments?
5. What disclosure language should be used so Buyer is clearly a principal and not acting as broker/agent?
6. Can assignment fees be shown and paid on the settlement statement?
7. If assignment fees are large, when should the transaction be structured as a double close instead?
8. What notice method is required for termination before closing?
